campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also heard public comments on various matters and announced that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on unspecified matter under government code
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 20, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution to identify certain properties in Saratoga and scheduled a public hearing for June 17, 2026, where residents can voice objections before the city makes a final decision on the matter.
keyTopics: Property identification initiative approved by council
Public hearing scheduled for June 17, 2026
Residents can submit protests before final approval

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve routine administrative items and set up the November general election for three open positions. Key decisions included confirming a new youth commissioner, approving a new housing development map, and authorizing the town manager to sign various service agreements.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 general municipal election scheduled for three open positions
New youth commissioner confirmed by selection committee
Final approval of Tract 10675 development map on Boulevard
Five-year software agreement with Accela, Inc. authorized
Budget authorization for revenue and expenditures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 8, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José city leaders held a public hearing on the city's spending plans for 2026-2027, including how much money different departments will receive and what services will cost residents. The council discussed both the operating budget (day-to-day expenses) and capital budget (big projects like infrastructure), plus any new or increased fees the city plans to charge.
keyTopics: 2026-2027 operating budget approval
Capital budget for city projects and infrastructure
New and increased fees and charges for residents and businesses
Public input on city spending priorities

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 4, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 4, 2026, that included both in-person and online participation options via Zoom. Residents could attend virtually or call in to watch and provide public comment on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access available via Zoom or phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants using raise hand feature
Phone unmuting instructions for speakers joining by telephone

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 8, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Personnel Board met to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes from August 2025 and honoring board member Jeff Kucer for his service. The meeting also included a flag salute and opened the floor for public comments on non-agenda items.
keyTopics: Recognition of service for Board Member Jeff Kucer
Approval of Personnel Board minutes from August 18, 2025
Public comment period on non-agenda items

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 2, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to recognize June as LGBTQ+ Pride Month and to acknowledge the United Nations World Environment Day on June 5, 2026. The council also opened the meeting for residents to speak about any city matters not on the formal agenda, with up to 30 minutes reserved for public comment.
keyTopics: LGBTQ+ Pride Month proclamation for June
UN World Environment Day recognition (June 5)
Public comment period for non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 8, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Economic Development & Marketing Committee held an in-person meeting on June 8, 2026. The city provided multiple ways for residents to participate, including Zoom video conferencing, webinar access, and phone dial-in options.
keyTopics: In-person Economic Development & Marketing Committee meeting
Remote participation options available (Zoom, webinar, phone)
Written public comment submission deadline: 11 AM day of meeting via email

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle routine business and make decisions about housing and community development funding. Key items included updating the city's five-year housing plan, reviewing council members' requests for new projects, and changing rules about how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Update to 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
Council members' new project requests and work plan items
Changes to the process for filling empty council seats
Approval of meeting minutes and calendar of upcoming sessions

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council discussed two main items: a proposed 3% increase to monthly storm and surface water drainage fees (raising costs to $18.14 per month for a typical home), and a separate discussion about electrification policies and potential subsidy programs.
keyTopics: Storm water drainage fee increase of 3% starting Fiscal Year 2027
Monthly utility bill impact: $18.14 per residential unit
Electrification state caps and discretionary subsidy options
